206665.explanation: Sounds noble, but we would have chosen the option 'nonsense' if available. This is outright unequal treatment. The statement implies that housing seekers who are parents with children, cleaners, civil servants, firefighters, and entrepreneurs are not urgent enough.

206666.explanation: Capelle is in a region where the need for social rental homes is great. Nonetheless, project developers focus on building homes in the expensive segment and owner-occupied homes with often 10% social rental. A strange situation where the coalition of Leefbaar Capelle, D66, VVD, and PvdA does not take responsibility.

206667.explanation: In the city center, high-rise buildings of limited height are allowed. The center lends itself well to that. The need for housing requires building, with building upwards almost the only option in our Capelle. The village character seems to be naturally overshadowed.

206669.explanation: There is good reason why low-income people qualify for various waivers. Waivers help stabilize the financial position of this group. Stopping waivers would bring greater costs to the municipality.

206670.explanation: Doing what is necessary for better air quality. That is partly possible when mopeds, scooters, cars, and other vehicles meet established standards regarding the emission of harmful substances.

206672.explanation: The use of electric cars calls for even more charging stations.

206673.explanation: Not everywhere. In built-up areas, city centers, and busy locations, a 30 km/h zone is worth considering. Not every traffic situation is suitable for a speed of 30 km/h. Traffic flow must also be taken into account. Is this speed appropriate for the traffic flow?

206674.explanation: Agreed, to also give entrepreneurs the opportunity to use facilities. Companies that are viable should definitely be able to use debt counseling, meaning the business remains operational by generating income itself to pay off debts.

206675.explanation: That is necessary to avoid rejecting applicants based on their last name. So that qualified Imelda, Joan, and Ahmed have equal opportunities as Johny and Anita. For WIJ Kleurrijk Capelle, it is self-evident that everyone counts; if only everyone saw it that way.

206676.explanation: Completely agree. Although targeted discrimination often happens subtly because it is punishable, fortunately, there are also cases where no discrimination occurs. Therefore, it is necessary to provide civil servants with skills to prevent these undesirable situations.

206678.explanation: The statement insinuates reluctance on the part of the municipality, which is not the case. As is known, the municipality faces a major financial responsibility, where choices must be made. WIJ Kleurrijk Capelle wants the municipality to make responsible, not emotional, choices.

206679.explanation: WIJ Kleurrijk Capelle embraces the current library concept that, besides lending books, also provides other social activities. Regarding longer opening hours—space for socializing—deviations can be made to existing surrounding facilities such as community centers.

206680.explanation: Capelle aan den IJssel has 125 nationalities among its residents. A cultural fund will support more cultural activities. Currently, a frequent complaint is that initiatives with a cultural character do not receive adequate attention and are even dismissed. That must change.

206681.explanation: Thanks in part to the Isala Theater, Capelle can hold its head high culturally. Cutting its budget would not do justice to citizens who warmly support culture. Reducing funding undermines its function as a meeting place and will also foster loneliness.

206682.explanation: The level of cultural facilities calls for such a provision. A solid level of facilities will also positively position Capelle regionally with a similar positive, attractive effect. We are in favor.

206683.explanation: If VVE is the solution. We embrace the focus on language disadvantage given its impact and repercussions in society. However, we advocate that as many parents with language disadvantages as possible overcome this backlog and pass it on to their children. A win-win.

206686.explanation: The energy-saving loan makes it possible for homeowners to make their homes somewhat more sustainably at a better price. Good for the environment and the wallet.

206687.explanation: A matter of decency. Therefore, we find it hard to imagine this is a serious statement. We realize that adultery can cause great harm, especially to families with children, and that government intervention is necessary. Fully agree to ban offensive advertising.

206688.explanation: With the current asylum accommodation, Capelle aan den IJssel contributes to the national demand. Not only is space limited in Capelle, but other municipalities must also contribute. Our position is not a rejection of 'people who do not look like us'; we follow the national government on this.

206689.explanation: Dispersal and small-scale shelter are not our preference. We therefore advocate maintaining the current form of accommodation at a single location.

206690.explanation: Asylum seekers who have exhausted all procedures should be accommodated centrally. That responsibility lies not with Capelle aan den IJssel but with the central government in The Hague and the minority cabinet of D66, VVD, and CDA. Let's see how humane they are.

206691.explanation: This is unequal treatment. It must apply to everyone who can still learn. It is necessary that every resident masters the language. Yet, in 2026, there are groups who still do not master the language. As a government, we are failing here, and the bill is indirectly paid.

206692.explanation: Considering the family composition, distinctions are already made in the amount of waste collection charges to be paid. It is hard enough; as a government, let's not make it harder for people. How do we compensate someone who produces more waste but, for example, is very economical with energy?

206694.explanation: It is especially important to inform citizens timely, accurately, and transparently about what to expect and what is affordable. Looking ahead to 2050, the plan is to connect to district heating or the (electric) heat pump. This year, residents will receive information about the plan.

206695.explanation: That depends on the balance of energy benefits and installation costs. Whether a wind turbine is profitable also depends on location, the generated wind energy, and the economic situation. It is worth investigating what this would mean for Capelle.

206696.explanation: Completely agree and necessary for safety.

206697.explanation: Sounds like a raid! Supervision orders follow a legal procedure and are not the municipality's responsibility. The municipality can collaborate supportively with CJG, Bureau Halt, youth work. OTS feels like a raid, like punishment. Not punishing but jointly looking at where things go wrong and how to support.

206698.explanation: We are not in favor of a casino or gambling hall in Capelle aan den IJssel, due to addiction, debts, and psychological problems resulting. We also thus prevent the attracting effect. Those who need a casino or gambling hall can, for example, go to Rotterdam.

206699.explanation: Absolutely; provided these homes meet the needs of the diverse target groups. We advocate assigning these homes only to regular resident home seekers. For other target groups, alternative housing seems most suitable. This way, we create a win-win situation.

206700.explanation: It is necessary to offer seniors suitable comfortable housing. Also good for the flow toward appropriate housing. We prefer that future building plans also offer space for suitable senior housing.

206701.explanation: In some municipalities, this already happens. That financial support mostly proves necessary due to limited budgets. Seniors often choose a smaller home so that families with children can move into the vacated home. A win-win situation.

206702.explanation: The Rotterdam Law is actually the Law on Special Measures for Metropolitan Problems. The law makes it possible to assign homes in vulnerable neighborhoods to relatively affluent households. Completely agree. This prevents metropolitan problems from concentrating further.

206703.explanation: This scheme already exists for welfare recipients and similar target groups who cannot afford education costs themselves. A small investment that helps people leave welfare. Those who can pay for education themselves should do so; personal responsibility.

206704.explanation: A great ambition to interest young people early in societal issues. They'll benefit later. This way, the municipality can further contribute to preventing and combating youth crime. Good plan.

206705.explanation: Completely agree; this already happens and should continue. A nice preparation for a permanent job to exit welfare and good for the work ethic.

206706.explanation: Earning a certain amount on the side is allowed. WIJ Kleurrijk supports this approach. Working while retaining benefits already happens and should continue. A good opportunity for daytime activities, earning own money and preventing loneliness.

206707.explanation: Yes and no. Important to properly accommodate the homeless. Where possible, preventing people from becoming homeless is the better option. We prefer that the municipality, in consultation with other municipalities like Rotterdam, offers shelter spaces in regional cooperation.

206709.explanation: What specific purpose should this serve? Building a viaduct can accelerate traffic flow. However, it’s questionable whether the costs and neighborhood appearance outweigh the traffic benefits. We think not.

206710.explanation: Free public transport for those 67 and older already exists and should be retained as far as we are concerned. We also advocate free public transport for children up to 12 years, as is already the case in other municipalities.

206711.explanation: More money is not the solution. The municipality should invest more in early detection and prevention of child poverty. Various provisions are already available for this target group: Youth Sports & Culture Fund, Stichting Leergeld, Think and Participate! Fund, and other regional funds.

206712.explanation: Children and pets depend on adults. Just as you don’t put a child out on the street, you don’t abandon a pet on the street when things get difficult. We want to prevent it from coming to the point where pets are abused or abandoned.

206713.explanation: A sex establishment is undesirable in Capelle. This form of prostitution evades rules and supervision by all means. The influx of customers and activities can give local residents a sense of insecurity. Facilitators are often involved in crime and undermining behavior.

206714.explanation: There are social institutions and entrepreneurs with whom the municipality can collaborate in co-creation. The municipality’s role can only be facilitative and not necessarily financial. The current presented financial position of the municipality is not suitable for this. The municipality is not a housing company.

206716.explanation: Considering the success rate of 60-75% after therapy, we tend towards: Agree. However, this is a private matter and not the municipality’s responsibility. Moreover, free relationship therapy is in some cases already possible.

206650.explanation: Advisory referenda are not the solution for complex political issues. Referenda are not binding. This otherwise causes confusion and dissatisfaction. A broad survey is a better alternative. The local government, as the final responsible guardian of the general interest, must make decisions.

206651.explanation: Capelle must balance expenses and income in the coming period. The Christian Union supports sound financial management and the identification and control of financial risks. Local taxes will not increase in principle more than the inflation correction.

206652.explanation: We observe that many small entrepreneurs have found it increasingly difficult and have little time for their families. Sometimes shopping center owners force stores to open on Sundays. Large retail chains compel some branches in Capelle to open on Sundays. The Christian Union opposes this expansion.

206653.explanation: Capelle is committed to meeting at least the regional agreements for housing status holders. As with the reception of Ukrainians, Capelle enables them to work within the regular labor process whenever possible.

206654.explanation: The Christian Union supports a replanting obligation. When a tree is removed, a tree must be replanted in the same spot or as close as possible.

206655.explanation: The Christian Union wants the municipality to only allow private households to have wood stoves equipped with wood smoke filters.

206656.explanation: The municipality provides residents with the opportunity to maintain their own neighborhood or street. The municipality provides subsidies for this purpose.

206657.explanation: The Christian Union strongly favors a general fireworks ban. A fireworks show on New Year's Eve is not the solution to the nuisance and violence against emergency workers during the day and night.

206659.explanation: The Christian Union supports that every resident of Capelle can participate in sports. The municipality contributes to the buildings and facilities of sports clubs but does not financially support clubs except through separate targeted subsidies.

206660.explanation: We naturally want to invest in safety, but it is not (yet) a given that more enforcement officers contribute to this. Prevention, neighborhood cooperation, and a strong neighborhood official are also powerful instruments.

206661.explanation: People are created and precious. They must not lose themselves, their freedom, and dignity through alcohol, drugs, or addiction. The Christian Union wants no coffee shops in Capelle; we want to actively combat drug use. The police must act firmly against all forms of nuisance caused by substance use.

206662.explanation: Camera surveillance is a good tool. The Christian Union wants to expand it smartly (proportionally) and continue to comply with privacy laws. The municipality regularly reviews policy and implementation and tries to improve them.

206663.explanation: Carrying a baton can provoke aggression, leading to more violence against enforcement officers instead of less. Instead, more investment should be made in training on verbal de-escalation and addressing people.

206664.explanation: The Christian Union believes everyone has the right to affordable housing. This especially applies to young people, elderly, starters, singles, and young families. We believe that in certain complexes, housing seekers from Capelle aan den IJssel should have priority, but certainly not for all homes.

206665.explanation: The Christian Union believes everyone has the right to affordable housing. This also applies to teachers, nurses, and police officers. We believe that in some complexes, these groups should be given priority, but certainly not in the allocation of all homes!

206666.explanation: The municipality of Capelle strives for at least 30% social housing in new construction plans. There are still long waiting lists for social rental housing, so the Christian Union supports this goal, but beyond that, more rental homes are needed for middle incomes!

206667.explanation: The Christian Union is cautious about adding high-rise buildings in Capelle. We only see possibilities for high-rise near public transport points and city edges. For high-rise, attention is needed for absorption in the neighborhood, livability, wind nuisance, daylight, parking, and fire safety.

206669.explanation: In situations where people want to pay but cannot, the municipality of Capelle aan den IJssel should collect payments socially. If someone has payment arrears, the municipality should consider that person's situation. Customized solutions are needed.

206670.explanation: The Christian Union wants to ban old polluting vehicles from central areas that do not meet the Euro IV standard. Mopeds/scooters in these areas will be electric only from 2030. From 2030, zero emissions will apply for deliveries. Garbage trucks should be emission-free. Explore city distribution!

206672.explanation: Gas stations in Capelle must provide fast charging for electric cars. We are investigating if this can be done on supermarket and shopping center parking lots. For people who cannot charge their car at home, there must be a public charger within 500 meters in Capelle: expand!

206673.explanation: In our view, the maximum speed within the built-up area should be 30 kilometers per hour on roads shared by cars and bicycles.

206674.explanation: The municipality of Capelle must also support entrepreneurs and self-employed workers (zzp’ers) with debts. Companies can apply for a social assistance credit (bbz-credit) with the municipality if they temporarily run into financial difficulties. The Christian Union believes the municipality should provide this support compassionately.

206675.explanation: The Christian Union is against discrimination and supports inclusion and diversity. Applying for jobs is about matching between the person and the organization. Previous experiences with this instrument at various government bodies showed it is not very effective in combating discrimination. Everyone is valuable and respect for others must exist everywhere, regardless of anything.

206676.explanation: Municipal civil servants must have sufficient knowledge and skills about unconscious biases, discrimination, antisemitism, and racism. Therefore, they undergo training that contributes to intercultural professionalism.

206678.explanation: Sports facilities must be nearby and affordable. It is great if people can develop their sports talents and even progress to top sports. Top-level sports are often an inspiration. Still, the municipality of Capelle should mainly focus on sports for everyone, nearby and affordable!

206679.explanation: The library plays an important role in promoting basic skills, encouraging reading pleasure, and supporting newcomers in learning the Dutch language. We will continue to invest in the library as an accessible and inclusive facility for all residents of Capelle.

206680.explanation: Children from low-income families can already get discounts or participate for free through schemes like the minimapas or the Youth Fund for Sports & Culture.

206681.explanation: The Christian Union wants residents to have access to culture, including the theater. Institutions such as the Isala Theater cost the municipality a lot of money but reach only a limited part of the population. Therefore, it must always be considered how cultural funds are spent.

206682.explanation: The Christian Union values culture and wants to maintain the role of existing facilities, such as the library and the Isala Theater. The Christian Union does not insist on creating an art route along the IJssel. Perhaps this is a great idea for a citizen’s initiative?

206683.explanation: Parents are responsible for upbringing. Society demands early learning. In case of (language) delays, consultative clinics inform parents that they can use pre- and early schooling facilities for appropriate education. The municipality encourages, arranges, and pays for this if parents cannot afford it.

206686.explanation: Homeowners are encouraged to make their homes more energy-efficient. Those with less financial means receive practical support and information about financial schemes they may qualify for.

206687.explanation: The Christian Union supports stable relationships and families as the basis for living together in the city. Commercial calls for adultery or websites that contribute to it do not fit with that. Therefore, the Christian Union supports a ban on advertising that promotes adultery.

206688.explanation: Newcomers must become part of our society as soon as possible. They must first learn the language, but this happens better if they immediately participate in society. The Christian Union advocates for dispersed, small-scale refugee reception. Recently arrived asylum seekers (AMA's) require our attention! Additional housing such as on Barbizonlaan is fine.

206689.explanation: The Christian Union supports spreading refugees. The municipality must offer a certain number of status holders housing as dictated by the national government and ensure this does not cause displacement. This can be achieved by providing (temporary) additional housing. Municipalities must make agreements with the province about this. No special asylum neighborhoods.

206690.explanation: The Christian Union believes everyone has the right to shelter. This also applies to rejected asylum seekers and stateless persons. As long as the external debt arrangement is insufficient or countries of origin do not cooperate adequately with returns, rejected asylum seekers must not suffer because of that.

206691.explanation: No obligation, but provide opportunities. We want newcomers to be integrated into our society as quickly as possible. The municipality encourages businesses to employ status holders. They must first learn the language, but this also proceeds faster and better when newcomers participate immediately.

206692.explanation: The municipality strives for less residual waste. We await the pilot with garbage bins. In recent years, we see behavioral changes: Capelle residents are more aware of the importance of waste separation to reduce residual waste.

206694.explanation: In the coming council period, clarity must emerge per neighborhood about the alternative to natural gas being worked towards. The municipality will organize a transparent process with opportunities for public input and then make the system choice.

206695.explanation: There is no place for wind turbines in our densely built municipality. The single wind turbine that already exists, at AVR next to the Van Brienenoord Bridge, is sufficient.

206696.explanation: The through cycle routes in Schollebos are well lit. This is important for residents’ safety. At the same time, we care for the welfare of our animals: they benefit from quiet at night. Therefore, not all paths are lit. This way, we ensure the preservation of greenery and safety.

206697.explanation: The family must be a safe and trusted basis for participating in society. Parenting is vulnerable and requires care for each other. Supervision is an existing child protection measure. The juvenile court can impose (provisional) supervision if there are serious concerns.

206698.explanation: The Christian Union opposes the establishment of casinos and gambling halls in Capelle aan den IJssel. The municipality must prevent addiction through education and health policies because people are too valuable. The risks of gambling are significant: especially young people can get into serious trouble as a result.

206699.explanation: The Christian Union considers an increase of up to 7,000 homes more than sufficient. Capelle aan den IJssel already ranks among the top 10 most densely populated municipalities in the Netherlands.

206700.explanation: The Christian Union wants the municipality of Capelle aan den IJssel to invest heavily in (adapted) homes and communal living arrangements for our elderly in the coming period. Communal living arrangements are important because they provide social contact, companionship, and neighborly help. Also, the flow of housi...

206701.explanation: The Christian Union focuses on increasing the supply of suitable housing for the elderly. To achieve this, construction must take place. It is also necessary to make existing homes suitable for lifelong living. Housing corporations should take responsibility for this in rental properties. Increasing options is more effective.

206702.explanation: As far as the Christian Union is concerned, the Rotterdam Act is unnecessary due to sufficient possibilities to allocate housing to different target groups through the current housing allocation system.

206703.explanation: The Christian Union emphasizes collaboration between the municipality, businesses, and (vocational) education to ensure a good connection between education and the labor market and to make it as easy as possible for job seekers to find suitable employment. The municipality actively encourages business establishment.

206704.explanation: The Christian Union wholeheartedly supports young people who want to fulfill social service time. However, the municipality has too few effective instruments to expand the possibilities to do this specifically in Capelle. The national government is responsible for this.

206705.explanation: The Christian Union supports a positive social assistance policy. We see people as entitled citizens, not as a cost factor. We support them in contributing to the neighborhood and municipality where they live. The municipality is a reliable partner that operates from trust. We advocate for simple social assistance and social cooperatives.

206706.explanation: In Capelle, it must be possible to follow a different type of reintegration trajectory in simple social assistance with low-regulation zones. For example, people should be able to work part-time. The municipality must clearly communicate which gifts or additional earnings are allowed within social assistance. Starting a business is possible!

206707.explanation: There is sufficient and appropriate shelter for homeless people. No one in Capelle aan den IJssel sleeps on the street against their will.

206709.explanation: The Christian Union prefers optimizing the existing metro line. In good consultation with RET and MRDH, the municipality must continue to work on limiting nuisance for residents. Elevating the metro line on pillars is a drastic and costly measure, irresponsible in a time of budget cuts.

206710.explanation: The municipality currently offers free public transport to all AOW (state pension) recipients, regardless of income level. That is a sympathetic provision, but many AOW recipients do not need this. We prefer free public transport for low-income households and children.

206711.explanation: Poverty often leads to social problems and poorer school performance. Poverty causes a lot of stress. The Christian Union wants to combat child poverty by providing financial support to families (allowances, subsidies, emergency funds).

206712.explanation: The Christian Union believes that the responsibility for keeping a pet lies with the owner. We consider a municipal awareness campaign patronizing and not a core task. Sellers and shelters already provide this information upon purchase or adoption. In case of nuisance, people should be addressed and, if necessary, fined.

206713.explanation: Prostitution is unequal. There is (almost) always inequality in power, money, or gender. Crime, human trafficking, exploitation, loneliness, and other social problems are the harsh reality behind the facade. For this reason, the Christian Union opposes prostitution.

206714.explanation: Investing in care community centers is not a government task. However, direct subsidies, incentive schemes for social activities, and support for sustainability measures are appropriate.

206716.explanation: In today’s culture, expectations for relationships are high. Divorce and breakup rates have increased significantly in recent decades. Efforts on support and prevention for relationship problems are needed.

206650.explanation: The CDA is fundamentally against referendums. Referendums reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice. This does not do justice to the complexity of many proposals. We have sufficient instruments to enable good participation.

206651.explanation: We want to keep the property tax (OZB) as low as possible. It directly affects the purchasing power of families and households. Therefore, we support a cap on OZB increases aligned with inflation. The OZB must not increase more than inflation in the coming period, assuming sufficient contributions from the national government.

206652.explanation: The current Sunday opening hours for shops remain. We do not want to change the 12:00 p.m. start time and remain opposed to any pressure on shopkeepers to open on Sundays against their will. The Sunday before Christmas is no exception.

206653.explanation: We will continue to fulfill our regional obligation to house status holders.

206654.explanation: The CDA wants to maintain or possibly extend the replanting obligation for trees on the green map. This applies to all 'unprotected' trees managed by the municipality. For private trees, we want to explore whether replanting or greening (duty of care) could be required.

206655.explanation: Living together means considering each other; both in terms of enjoyment and nuisance. The municipality cannot ban wood stoves. The Environmental Act allows setting rules for the use of wood stoves. Enforcement in cases of nuisance or irresponsible use is important.

206656.explanation: We believe in the strength of people. Initiatives from residents who want to make their street, neighborhood, or district more beautiful, safe, or livable deserve support and are not to be hindered. We maintain Maak Capelle as a fund to support these initiatives for community cohesion and livability.

206657.explanation: We celebrate New Year's Eve together. That shows solidarity and community. Capelle can contribute by, for example, organizing a show at the Algerabrug. This supports culture and tradition.

206659.explanation: The CDA advocates for accessible and affordable sports facilities so that sports are available to everyone. The volunteer-driven association life is the glue of society. We support sports clubs and volunteers who play a crucial role in the community.

206660.explanation: We advocate for the return of the BOA (special enforcement officers) in public transport. Safety is a prerequisite for a strong community. For the CDA, safety means not only appropriate enforcement but also a reliable government that protects vulnerable people and prevents people from falling through the cracks.

206661.explanation: The municipality should take the lead in working with owners and tenants to ensure attractive and viable shopping areas in every neighborhood. We oppose the arrival of gambling halls, sex shops, and coffee shops in Capelle.

206662.explanation: Camera surveillance can be helpful in combating crime and identifying perpetrators. Especially in the outskirts and at the municipal boundaries, camera surveillance should be expanded.

206663.explanation: Support for the BOAs. The CDA supports bodycams and also batons. These should become standard equipment for (green) BOAs for their safety. A previous application for batons was rejected by the ministry. If a new application is made, the CDA will support it.

206664.explanation: We want to strengthen local ties by giving priority to Capelle’s youth and elderly.

206665.explanation: We focus on appropriate requirements for housing and prevent arbitrarily imposed demands, such as a professional group excluded from the designated housing stock.

206666.explanation: We will accelerate housing construction, with at least 30% social rental housing. We focus on Capelle’s needs in the proportion of new homes. We strive for the construction of mid-range rental and owner-occupied homes accessible to everyone.

206667.explanation: More high-rise residential buildings are allowed in the city center. For the CDA, they must not be taller than the town hall. No sacrifice of green spaces, and attention to (young) historical buildings (e.g., Kerklaan area).

206669.explanation: A considerable group lives below the poverty line or has an income of at most 120-130% of the legal social minimum. Although this percentage decreases due to income developments such as minimum wage increases, there is an increase in problematic debts.

206670.explanation: During the treatment of the Capelle Area Vision, the CDA opposed the introduction of an environmental zone. For the CDA, entrepreneurs must be ready for this.

206672.explanation: We want to expand the network of charging points for electric cars, preferably with fast chargers.

206673.explanation: The CDA is not in favor of a 30 km/h speed limit everywhere in Capelle. However, it is appropriate in residential neighborhoods or around the center. But not on main roads, such as Abram van Rijckevorselweg.

206674.explanation: Capelle entrepreneurs can already ask questions or express concerns about financial matters at the Regional Office for Self-Employed. Conditions for possible (financial) support for starting, established, and closing entrepreneurs are listed on the Rotterdam municipal website.

206675.explanation: Municipalities play a key role in creating an environment where diversity is valued. Where everyone can be themselves, regardless of sexual orientation, ethnicity, disability, or other personal characteristics. We ensure everyone feels seen and that we do justice to differences.

206676.explanation: Municipalities set an example in creating an environment where diversity is valued. Everyone can be themselves, regardless of sexual orientation, ethnicity, disability, or other personal characteristics. We honor differences and want to ensure that no one is overlooked.

206678.explanation: The plan has already been approved in the 2026 budget. The CDA voted in favor. We preserve green spaces and encourage physical activity. We invest in public squares, sports, and cultural facilities that invite playing, socializing, and moving.

206679.explanation: We promote social encounters among people through dialogue weeks and generational connections via, among others, community centers, churches, schools, or libraries. We strengthen digital skills by co-facilitating the library. We want a library staff member dedicated to language skills for newcomers.

206680.explanation: The CDA supports maintaining subsidies for cultural initiatives and projects, such as visual arts, performing arts, music education, libraries, or local broadcasting. Also for the continuation of Maak Capelle, which supports residents’ initiatives, including applications for cultural projects.

206681.explanation: We strengthen the community. We encourage social interaction among people. We support culture and tradition, such as the Sinterklaas arrival. Events and culture must represent everyone, with attention to diverse traditions.

206682.explanation: Plans already exist to strengthen the IJssel zone. Several artworks are already located along the IJssel. The CDA is positive about an art route. As CDA, we invest in public squares, sports, and cultural amenities that invite playing, meeting, and moving.

206683.explanation: We want targeted actions aimed at parents to encourage participation in early childhood education (ages 2.5 to 4), so children start group 1 better prepared. In addition, we collaborate with schools and partnerships on a safety net to prevent children from staying at home.

206686.explanation: We actively support local energy cooperatives and assist residents with energy-saving measures. We back pilot projects where small-scale, bold solutions for energy problems can be developed.

206687.explanation: The CDA wants to handle advertisements for (online) gambling cautiously, as these may lead to suicide, and within the framework of good morals, ads promoting infidelity. We also want to examine ads for cannabis use and coffee shops.

206688.explanation: We take responsibility for asylum seeker reception under the dispersal law. The quality of reception is paramount. We must consider the limits of society’s capacity; otherwise, we cannot fulfill the promise of good integration.

206689.explanation: The goal is to offer refugees a safe place in our society. At the same time, we remain mindful of the needs of all residents. It is a dual responsibility that brings tensions. The quality of reception is leading.

206690.explanation: Responsibility and solidarity guide our efforts to provide refugees a safe place. At the same time, we remain aware of all residents’ needs. It is a dual responsibility that brings tensions. Rejected asylum seekers must leave the country.

206691.explanation: We focus on integrating new Dutch citizens. We make participation in Dutch language lessons mandatory and pay special attention to strengthening digital skills, for example by co-facilitating such activities through the library.

206692.explanation: We want to combat waste tourism in cooperation with the region and implement a pass system for underground waste bins. We promote awareness campaigns for better waste separation and less litter. Payment per bag only when it can be introduced throughout Capelle.

206694.explanation: The CDA advocates for a livable environment. We believe in collaboration with the community so that sustainable initiatives are locally supported. The transition to a climate-friendly municipality requires involvement from everyone, with the municipality facilitating and supporting.

206695.explanation: We prevent building large wind turbines that may negatively affect residents’ enjoyment of living. We support local energy cooperatives and assist residents with energy-saving resources. We back pilot projects where small-scale, bold solutions can be developed.

206696.explanation: We want to improve safety on bike paths within built-up areas. For Schollebos, lighting must balance with nature (for example, using moonlight spectrum or motion sensors). This means further research is needed first to find the best solution.

206697.explanation: We fully focus on prevention in tackling (youth) crime. This will allow more room for youth workers, supervisors, and other initiatives to keep young people on the right path and maintain connections with each other. We welcome the declaration of intent from the Care and Safety Chamber.

206698.explanation: We oppose the opening of gambling halls, sex shops, and coffee shops. It is important to realize that the economy is not only about profit but also about the well-being of all residents, with special attention to vulnerable groups (ensuring livelihood security and combating poverty).

206699.explanation: It is important that neighborhoods are green, blue, and sustainable, with good nearby amenities and attention to quality of life. The municipality must take a leading role in future-proof residential neighborhoods. We want research into aligning needs with housing development plans.

206700.explanation: To counter loneliness and fragmentation of care, we want to invest in clustered living arrangements focused on social interaction, such as senior courtyards, the return of modern retirement homes (2.0), multi-generational courtyards, and informal care homes.

206701.explanation: The CDA wants to continue the existing 'Van Groot naar Beter' scheme by Havensteder. Older renters receive active support in finding suitable housing with an incentive premium. The scheme runs until the end of 2026. If it effectively promotes housing flow, it should continue beyond 2026.

206702.explanation: The CDA was already against the Rotterdam Act; the waterbed effect is now visible in other (vulnerable) neighborhoods. More focus on local ties by prioritizing Capelle youth and elderly (housing flow). More interventions within neighborhoods themselves beyond simply assigning housing seekers with certain professions.

206703.explanation: We actively facilitate social initiatives that contribute to integration, such as language buddies, job guidance, volunteer projects, and tutoring. We support involved organizations, associations, and churches for this purpose.

206704.explanation: We support new places for volunteer work, such as companies (employee volunteering) and social service time, through which we offer young people tools to contribute to Capelle society.

206705.explanation: As a municipality, we do provide internships and work experience places and call on our entrepreneurs to make such opportunities available in their businesses.

206706.explanation: Through IJssel municipalities, Capelle residents with medical hour restrictions already have possible additional exemptions. We welcome (volunteer) work combined with benefit retention as meaningful daytime activities or prospects for a permanent job.

206707.explanation: Better regional shelter than wandering and sleeping in our neighborhoods. Homeless shelters are an essential part of assistance in the Rijnmond region. If the municipality must contribute more financially to regional homeless shelters, the CDA is positive about this.

206709.explanation: Full commitment is required; we cannot wait decades any longer. Research shows that by 2040, with all housing plans for Capelle, Kralingseweg and metro crossings will be negative outliers in accessibility throughout the day, 7:00 am to 8:00 pm. Waiting times and congestion will increase significantly.

206710.explanation: The CDA supports retaining the current free public transport scheme for all 67+ seniors. It is also noted that free travel is permitted outside Capelle within a certain area. Research shows that free public transport for all 67+ seniors is cheaper for the municipality than an income-dependent system.

206711.explanation: 3.1% of our residents live below the poverty line. 28% have an income of a maximum 120-130% of the statutory social minimum. We invest in equal opportunities by supporting education for all and additional support for children in poverty and with disabilities.

206712.explanation: This is already included in the Animal Welfare policy. Information about responsible pet ownership will be added to the municipal website. The knowledge level of aid agencies and district teams will be increased regarding recognizing signs of animal abuse and neglect and how to act.

206713.explanation: The CDA wants the municipality to enforce more strictly against human trafficking and exploitation. But also to ensure neighborhoods and districts are livable and safe.

206714.explanation: We do not build or manage housing ourselves as a municipality. We support collective senior housing facilities in close consultation with, among others, housing corporations and care institutions so that elderly can live comfortably. We support cooperative initiatives by individuals such as senior courtyards.

206716.explanation: Parents or guardians are primarily responsible for this. If you cannot resolve it together, you can seek help alone or with family. CJG and Welzijn Capelle provide training and assistance related to divorce. What other forms of “therapy” should the municipality fund?

206651.explanation: Businesses and more capable individuals can contribute more to property tax (ozb).

206656.explanation: Green maintenance is a municipal responsibility.

206659.explanation: Depending on the cause, the municipality assesses how the association can be helped.

206662.explanation: The municipality places cameras only where this improves safety.

206663.explanation: With the current responsibilities of the public order officers (boa's), we believe it is not necessary.

206666.explanation: Even 35% is too low. The SP wants 46%.

206673.explanation: We keep the main roads at 50 km/h.

206690.explanation: If they are not taken in, they disappear into illegality.

206696.explanation: There are too few vehicles in the forest for social control.

206699.explanation: As long as 46% are affordable homes.

206705.explanation: A job comes with a fair wage.

206650.explanation: Although a referendum costs a lot of money and time, we believe a referendum is justifiable on important issues. In addition, we think neighborhoods should be more actively involved in advising on topics, for example via neighborhood surveys.

206651.explanation: Currently, the property tax (ozb) in Capelle is set according to the rules and is certainly not too high. If the government receives less money, less can be done. Therefore, maintaining the current system and amount is appropriate.

206652.explanation: We believe that is unnecessary. The market can indicate that well on its own, and what we hear now is that most shops simply close their doors on Sundays on time. Supermarkets, on the other hand, already have more extended Sunday opening hours.

206653.explanation: We believe everyone should be treated equally and humanely, including refugees with a residence permit. It is therefore unacceptable to us that residents of Capelle have to wait for social housing because of this. There are already enough urgency declarations within our own municipality.

206654.explanation: A good goal to maintain the number of trees! We wholeheartedly support this. Trees provide oxygen and a habitat for various animal species.

206655.explanation: Laws and regulations regarding emissions ensure companies offer solutions to combat pollution, such as carbon filters and the like. We believe in that. Wood stoves and open fireplaces provide atmosphere and warmth and are not necessarily less harmful than large power plants.

206656.explanation: If residents believe they can maintain their neighborhood better than the municipality, it should be possible. A reimbursement can be given for purchasing materials, such as plants and baskets, but not for labor, in our view.

206657.explanation: Events promote togetherness. A fireworks show on New Year's Eve certainly belongs to that, in our opinion!

206659.explanation: Sports clubs can experience fluctuating membership numbers. If a sports club is at risk of failing due to temporarily fewer members, but there is a prospect that numbers will rise again, the municipality should help.

206660.explanation: Visible enforcement on the street sends a signal. This signal is important and creates trust and a strengthened sense of safety.

206661.explanation: There is no place for a coffee shop in Capelle.

206662.explanation: Camera surveillance is currently deployed where needed. Less or more surveillance is therefore not the issue. We want targeted use of camera surveillance where it is necessary.

206663.explanation: Carrying a baton has no added value for us. Enforcement officers already have handcuffs and pepper spray. The difference with the police should exist and remain, in our view.

206664.explanation: We think it is important to have and maintain strong neighborhoods. Togetherness with an eye on preserving social contacts and communities is important in this.

206665.explanation: We believe every resident of Capelle should have equal opportunities in the rental housing market. Starters on the housing market have just as much right to a home as certain other target groups.

206666.explanation: Capelle now applies a minimum percentage of 30% for social rental housing. That does not need to be higher as a basic standard in our view. Specific agreements should be made within each project, with a minimum of 30% social rental housing, but it can be higher.

206667.explanation: This is allowed, provided it fits in the environment. The city center of Capelle is the ideal place for this in combination with amenities such as the metro and shopping center. Let's not forget that Capelle is not New York; everything must fit Capelle.

206669.explanation: We must continue helping people who have to make ends meet financially and make financial choices, such as between having food or paying the waste disposal tax.

206670.explanation: Polluting vehicles (except classic cars) are already becoming less common. Larger companies have already replaced their fleets. Therefore, it is not necessary for us to establish an environmental zone in Capelle’s small city center.

206672.explanation: Electric driving may be the future, but in daily practice people are inconvenienced by reserved parking spaces without an electric car. In our view, parking spaces can be made hybrid if necessary, but not more electric spaces.

206673.explanation: Where there is no crossing bicycle and pedestrian traffic, the speed limit should just be 50 km/h. The Capelseweg or Burgemeester Bakkerlaan are examples. Here, 30 km/h is nonsensical.

206674.explanation: This depends entirely on the situation. If an entrepreneur has run the business improperly, in our view they should stop the business. If it is outside the entrepreneur's fault, debt assistance should be possible.

206675.explanation: Anonymous applications sometimes cause time loss and unnecessary surprises. Of course, people should be hired based on their abilities and motivation, but you can also simply include your name and a photo.

206676.explanation: This always seems good to us. People at the municipality's information desk must all be treated equally. Article 1 of the constitution also provides for this.

206678.explanation: Our sports facilities must be in order. If danger threatens, for example, due to collapse, action must be taken promptly. The construction of the Sporthal Schenkel has already been decided and must be carried out quickly.

206679.explanation: It is a great idea if multiple meeting possibilities are created in the library and combined with reading. Not only in our city center but also, for example, in neighborhoods like the former Schollevaar library. It brings liveliness.

206680.explanation: It is a good idea for a municipality to spend money on culture for young and old. This cultural fund must be managed by professionals from the sector, so good decisions can be made based on objectives to which the fund grants and to which it does not.

206681.explanation: The Isala Theater in its current form requires support from the municipality. Of course, it would be nice if it could exist financially independently and autonomously; however, various activities and performances for Capelle are organized there without a profit motive.

206682.explanation: Art is a nice addition to public space, but we do not think the municipality should realize this. Perhaps it can be funded through a cultural fund.

206683.explanation: It is important to pay sufficient attention to language delays. We do not think the municipality needs to actively encourage this; parents already understand this well themselves and usually already know where to go via the CJG.

206686.explanation: There are now numerous schemes, both from businesses and banks. The municipality's role is to offer a financial incentive to save energy until the market takes over. This is currently the case.

206687.explanation: This is regulated nationally by the Advertising Code Committee. As local government, we should not concern ourselves with this.

206688.explanation: The municipality of Capelle is one of the most densely populated in the Netherlands. Our shelter on Barbizonlaan, as well as at the Rivium, is sufficient according to national agreements. Expansion is not desired.

206689.explanation: Distribution seems basically good, but has a big impact in our neighborhoods for a densely populated area like Capelle. Combined with the faltering guidance these people receive, this is not desired in our districts and neighborhoods.

206690.explanation: If asylum seekers do not receive status in our country, they must be accommodated elsewhere. This should be arranged by the national government; we should not want to solve this in Capelle.

206691.explanation: By learning the language (and customs) of a country, you increase your chances on the labor market. It is therefore in everyone's interest to learn the language as soon as possible.

206692.explanation: Provided it is not business waste, people should be free to use the option to dispose of residual waste.

206694.explanation: This should be arranged and tackled nationally. As Capelle, we should not spend municipal time and energy on this.

206695.explanation: Wind turbines belong at sea and not in densely populated areas!

206696.explanation: This is not necessary. The Schollebos covers only 1.15 km2. Feelings of insecurity can also be avoided by cycling a little detour. Too much lighting disrupts flora and fauna.

206697.explanation: If parents cannot keep their children in check, decisive action must be taken. Professional help is then needed to protect the well-meaning resident of Capelle.

206698.explanation: This does not belong in Capelle.

206699.explanation: We will not solve the housing crisis as Capelle. The 5,000 homes in Rivium are, as far as we are concerned, the last major development. Let’s then focus on the existing neighborhoods and ensure the existing districts, facilities and (rental) homes are renovated.

206700.explanation: It is important that our elderly do not live in loneliness. The importance of these housing forms for the elderly is finally back on the agenda. However, this costs money and often does not directly benefit owners and developers. The municipality must step in here.

206701.explanation: It is important that the municipality supports this. A house freed up for a new family and a suitable home for an elderly person is a win-win situation.

206702.explanation: Abolishing it now means facing the consequences again in a few years. The Rotterdam law is successful, so let’s continue with it.

206703.explanation: We do not consider this a primary task of the municipality. Retraining is paid for by companies, the Employee Insurance Agency (UWV), or the government.

206704.explanation: Social service time creates engagement. Commitment to society only produces winners. For example, young people helping the elderly with groceries or other tasks.

206705.explanation: It is good that everyone can participate; this creates social contacts and gives people a rhythm. You don’t just get social assistance benefits, but if work can be provided, that would bring many additional social benefits.

206706.explanation: Everyone participates and counts! Work also offers social contacts and the feeling of being useful. This prevents isolation and depression.

206707.explanation: No one should have to sleep on the street. This, however, must be reviewed and arranged properly on a regional level.

206709.explanation: Car, bicycle and pedestrian traffic should not cross with the metro. It causes delays, such as for the IJsselland Hospital, and also creates unsafe situations for pedestrians trying to catch the metro. In our view, the metro should be underground, but on stilts is a good alternative.

206710.explanation: Being able to travel for free gets elderly people outside, visiting each other and various facilities. In our opinion, this is a key to preventing loneliness among older people.

206711.explanation: Children should not live in poverty. They have the right to a good life and cannot be the victims of the situation their parents have put them in. If children are helped, parents can breathe and look for solutions again.

206712.explanation: A campaign costs money. Using common sense and visiting a good webpage on the municipal website can achieve the same goal.

206713.explanation: As far as we know, Capelle has not issued permits for sex workers. But Capelle should not issue permits for this either. A person is not an object to be used.

206714.explanation: The quality of the elderly housing complexes is not good. Elderly people do not feel safe and care is substandard for various reasons, such as staffing. If the municipality can guarantee that it can manage this properly, then that is definitely a good option.

206716.explanation: The municipality should not offer free mediation. You can also talk about this with your loved ones, general practitioner (POH), and you can even receive psychological help if you cannot communicate well due to a psychological disorder.

statements.title: Residents who produce a lot of residual waste should pay higher waste collection fees.

titleParts.glossary: Waste that remains after paper, plastic and organic waste have been collected separately.
Municipal tax for collecting and processing household waste.

statements.title: The municipality should extend shop opening hours on Sundays.
Capelle aan den IJssel should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

Asylum reception in our municipality must not be expanded.

statements.title: The municipality should build its own community care centers where older people can also live.
The municipality should retain the energy-saving loan for homeowners.

statements.title: The municipality should make a plan for each neighborhood for a clear alternative to natural gas.
Capelle aan den IJssel should introduce an environmental zone to keep polluting vehicles out of the center.

statements.moreInfo: Not all cycle paths in the Schollebos in the Schollevaar district have lighting. There is debate about whether they should, or whether existing lighting should actually be dimmed more often.

statements.title: At least 35% of new homes built in Capelle aan den IJssel should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

statements.title: Capelle aan den IJssel should allow the establishment of a casino or gambling hall.
The municipality should ban advertising in public spaces that encourages adultery.

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.
Having a (sexual) relationship with someone other than one's steady partner, without that partner's consent.

statements.title: The municipality should stick to the plan to realize 10,000 new homes in Capelle aan den IJssel.
The municipality should require residents on welfare who do not speak Dutch well to take a language course.
The municipality should promote that more young people complete a community service year in Capelle aan den IJssel.

titleParts.glossary: A period in which young people (ages 12–30) voluntarily help others to learn and develop their talents, for example at a sports club or in elderly care.

titleParts.text: in Capelle aan den IJssel gaan vervullen.

statements.title: The municipality should provide financial support to elderly people who want to move to a more suitable home.
The municipality should invest more money in homeless shelters so there are always enough places.
The municipality should fund an awareness campaign for prospective pet owners about the responsibilities of owning a pet.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld steken in een

titleParts.glossary: A series of advertisements to inform people about a topic and encourage them to think or change.

statements.title: People aged 67 and over should be able to travel free on public transport in Capelle aan den IJssel, at the municipality's expense.
The municipality should establish a local cultural fund to support cultural initiatives and projects.

titleParts.glossary: Money set aside for culture, commonly called a "pot."
Ideas from citizens to improve their neighborhood, street or district.

statements.title: The municipality should investigate whether the metro line at Schenkel can be elevated on pillars.
statements.moreInfo: The metro track at De Schenkel is formally located on Rotterdam territory. Any study into placing it on stilts will therefore have to be carried out by Capelle aan den IJssel together with the municipality of Rotterdam.

statements.title: The municipality should offer residents on welfare a basic job while allowing them to keep their benefits.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet inwoners met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
A job with social benefit, intended for someone who has difficulty finding other work.

statements.title: The municipality should stop issuing permits to sex workers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het verstrekken van

titleParts.glossary: Permission from the municipality to do something.
A person who offers sexual services or acts for payment.

statements.title: The Rotterdam Act should be abolished in Capelle aan den IJssel.

titleParts.glossary: With the Rotterdam Act (Law on special measures for major urban problems) the municipality, together with housing corporations, can set requirements including income and education for people who want to rent a social rental home.

titleParts.text: moet in Capelle aan den IJssel worden afgeschaft.

statements.moreInfo: The Rotterdam Act allows the municipality to temporarily impose stricter requirements on new residents. People with low incomes or a history of nuisance can, for example, be excluded. The aim of the law is to improve the livability of neighborhoods.

statements.title: Residents of Capelle aan den IJssel should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Capelle aan den IJssel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality should make funds available to citizens who want to maintain the greenery in their neighborhood themselves.
The municipality should create an art route along the IJssel.
The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should offer free couples therapy to parents who cannot resolve their conflicts together.
When allocating social rental housing, teachers, nurses and police officers should be given priority.
The municipal enforcement officers who monitor safety in Capelle aan den IJssel should be given batons.
The municipality should reduce the subsidy to the Isala Theater.
The use of open fireplaces and wood stoves should remain permitted in Capelle aan den IJssel.
Municipal civil servants should be able to apply anonymously for internal vacancies.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show every New Year's Eve.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The speed limit within built-up areas must be set to 30 km/h everywhere.
The municipality should start a pilot to place parents under supervision if their child repeatedly engages in criminal behavior.
More high-rise residential buildings should be allowed in the city center.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
Capelle aan den IJssel should continue to provide shelter to asylum seekers who are not permitted to stay in the Netherlands.
Entrepreneurs must be able to apply for debt counseling while keeping their business.
The municipality must support dispersed, small-scale shelter for refugees.
There should be less camera surveillance in Capelle aan den IJssel.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.
Residents who take a course or training to improve their chances of finding a job in the city should have their costs reimbursed by the municipality.
The municipality should commit to the rapid construction of a new Schenkel sports hall.
Residents with a disability who receive social assistance should be able to do paid work or volunteer while retaining their benefits.
The library should become a broad community meeting place with extended opening hours.
The municipality should invest more money in combating child poverty.
The municipality should actively encourage parents of young children with a language delay to use free early childhood education (VVE).
The municipality should provide funding for communal living arrangements for the elderly.
